title: Staged Trainer: Curriculum Ordering for Fastest Convergence
strategies: as-shipped order: cycle the examples in the shuffled arrival order (reproduces the evaluator's weak q_base baseline, ~0.1)
static difficulty sort: order examples easy-to-hard by concept layer and cycle that fixed foundation-first curriculum, so prerequisites precede dependents within each pass

state-aware myopic greedy: simulate the frozen student from the public dynamics and at each step show the example with the largest immediate loss reduction LR*readiness*(1-m_c), adaptively front-loading foundations only until they unlock dependents

search / lookahead: seed a schedule from difficulty-sort or greedy, then locally perturb (swap/insert re-shows, beam or rollout over the deterministic simulator) to shave updates toward -- but never reaching -- the prerequisite-free optimistic bound
